articles.title: Report: Iran rescues over a dozen bulk carrier crew after collision in Hormuz Strait
articles.summary: Report: Iran rescues over a dozen bulk carrier crew after collision in Hormuz Strait Iran reportedly rescued 23 foreign crew members north of Qesh Island in the Strait of Hormuz, according to the semi-official Fars news agency. The incident occurred after a bulk carrier collided with another vessel in the waterway. The bulk carrier suffered serious damage as a result and began taking on water.  This prompted the captain to order an emergency evacuation. Fars reported that crew onboard were safely transferred to Qeshm Island.

articles.title: IRGC says US air base in Jordan targeted
articles.summary: IRGC says US air base in Jordan targeted The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) said it targeted a US air base in Jordan, Fars News reported. The statement called on the Jordanian people to dismantle US bases in their country. "You know very well that not only do we not have any enmity with your country, but we also love you, the noble people, who understand the pain and oppression of the Palestinian people more than any other nation," the IRGC said.

When asked how he felt looking at the state of Gaza, Katz said: "A good feeling, isn’t it?" The defence minister expressed that this was an outcome of a "deliberate policy" aimed at removing threats, adding that Israel left behind its previous strategy of carrying out temporary military raids. articles.title: IRGC attacks target US Fifth Fleet in Bahrain
articles.summary: IRGC attacks target US Fifth Fleet in Bahrain Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) claimed missile and drone strikes targeting the US Fifth Fleet in Bahrain. According to the IRGC, the attacks set ablaze fuel depots at the site and destroyed several radars, including a Patriot radar, the fleet’s air control radar and a C-RAM early warning radar system. It also claimed that the control and monitoring centre for unmanned surface vessels was also destroyed. The IRGC added that the "retaliatory operation continues".

articles.title: Australia Sees No Mass Layoffs From AI Just Yet
articles.summary: A first-of-its-kind report by the Australian government finds AI has not yet caused broad disruption in the nation’s labor market. That’s as the rising demand for AI is heightening concerns over the future of white-collar jobs. Scientia Professor of AI at the University of New South Wales Toby Walsh joins "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" to discuss why Canberra needs to act now to mitigate the impact of AI and prepare workers for change. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.summary: Trump reiterates that US attacks brought Iran back to 'the Stone Ages' US president Donald Trump said that Iran's "power has been largely taken away" following US strikes on the country.  "Now, they have something left, but it’s been largely taken away. As an example, their military had 159 ships; 159 ships are under the sea," he told Newsmax. Trump also claimed that all of Tehran's "aeroplanes are all gone. Their radars gone, their anti-aircraft is gone, their weapon-making capability is largely gone – about 84 percent – and, you know, it’s largely gone". "So they are a much differe

articles.title: Centcom says US launched attacks across Iran
articles.summary: Centcom says US launched attacks across Iran The latest wave of US attacks across Iran were "completed", Central Command (Centcom) said in a statement. Centcom indicated that US forces "successfully struck military targets" during the five-hour operation, including ones in Bushehr, Chah Bahar, Jask, Konarak, Abu Musa, and Bandar Abbas. "CENTCOM forces employed precision munitions against Iranian coastal defense systems, missile and drone sites, and maritime capabilities," the statement added.

articles.title: IRGC claim attack on two vessels in Hormuz Strait, targets Bahrain's Juffair base
articles.summary: IRGC claim attack on two vessels in Hormuz Strait, targets Bahrain's Juffair base Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) said that two "offending supertankers" were hit and disabled in the Strait of Hormuz. According to the IRGC, the vessels were struck after they ignored warning, turned off navigation systems and attempted to pass through "a mined route". The statement added that the US was "inciting vessels to use an illegal route", warning that cooperation with the "aggressor enemy" would result in damage, delays in reopening the waterway and a global energy cri

articles.title: Vessel hit by unknown projectile 40 nautical miles from Oman's Qalhat, maritime body says
articles.summary: Vessel hit by unknown projectile 40 nautical miles from Oman's Qalhat, maritime body says A tanker was hit by an unknown projectile while travelling 40 nautical miles north east of Oman's Qalhat, the United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations agency (UKMTO) said.  The master of the vessel reported that the projectile hit the starboard-side engine room, adding that all crew members were safe.

articles.title: UAE says two tankers hit by Iranian missiles in Hormuz Strait, killing crew member
articles.summary: UAE says two tankers hit by Iranian missiles in Hormuz Strait, killing crew member The UAE Ministry of Defence said that two national vessels were targeted by two Iranian cruise missiles in the southern lane of the Strait of Hormuz, killing one Indian crew member and wounding eight others - including four seriously. According to the statement, the attack occurred in Omani territorial waters. Fires that broke out on both tankers had since been brought under control.  The ministry condemned what it called a "blatant attack, which is considered a serious violation and a clear breach of

articles.title: Trumps says he wants Gulf states to reimburse US for 'protection'
articles.summary: Trumps says he wants Gulf states to reimburse US for 'protection' US President Donald Trump said he wanted Gulf countries to reimburse Washington for helping "protect the Strait of Hormuz". "I want to be reimbursed because we’re protecting a very rich portion of the world. We’re spending money, so… we are going to be reimbursed for protection," Trump told reporters in the Oval Office, mentioning Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Qatar, Bahrain and Kuwait as countries the US has protected.

articles.title: Explosion heard in Iran's Bushehr, four wounded in US attack on Omidiyeh
articles.summary: Explosion heard in Iran's Bushehr, four wounded in US attack on Omidiyeh Fresh blasts were heard in Iran's southern city of Bushehr, according to the semi-official Mehr news agency.  Elsewhere, areas struck by the US in the south western city of Omidiyeh resulted in the wounding of at least four people.

articles.title: Fresh US hits on Iran, despite Trump saying a deal with Tehran 'possible'
articles.summary: Fresh US hits on Iran, despite Trump saying a deal with Tehran 'possible' Several areas in south western Iran’s city of Omidiyeh were hit by US projectiles, the semi-official news agency Mehr reported. The fresh US salvo is the latest escalation in spite of remarks by US President Donald Trump suggesting that a deal with Tehran was still possible. Earlier, Trump told reporters: "Yeah, I think a deal is possible. Sure, I do." "We had a deal with them two days ago and then they said 'Oh we can't make that deal. We have to negotiate it further'," he added.

articles.title: Iran warns it would not allow US to 'interfere' in Hormuz Strait
articles.summary: Iran warns it would not allow US to 'interfere' in Hormuz Strait Iran's military said that it would not allow the United States to "interfere" in the management of the Strait of Hormuz as hostilities with Washington resumed. The spokesperson for the Khatam al-Anbiya military command, Ebrahim Zolfaghari, said in a video message also warned Gulf countries that any cooperation with Washington would be considered an "act of war".
